summary: The test suite covers core functionality (matrix generation, include/exclude rules, size validation, configuration options) with 18 well-organized Pester tests. However, significant gaps exist: no explicit 3+ dimensional matrix tests (the general case), missing edge cases like malformed exclude rules, duplicate dimension values, null values, special characters, single-dimension matrices, and deterministic ordering verification. Tests validate error throwing but not error message content. Test fixtures are minimal and inline with no helper library. Most tests exercise happy paths; rigorous edge case coverage is weak. Design is clean with good naming conventions and proper Pester structure, but lacks comments explaining test strategy and approach. The separate test-harness.ps1 using act is integration-level testing, not unit testing—creates inconsistency with unit test philosophy. Would catch obvious regressions but misses subtle bugs in boundary conditions and unusual input combinations.
